headline: 4 smart contracts discovered: xDAI Bridge proxy, implementation, and 3/4 multisig governance on Ethereum mainnet

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search identified xDAI Bridge proxy at 0x4aa42145Aa6Ebf72e164C9bBC74fbD3788045016 (labeled 'Gnosis Chain: xDai Bridge') and associated multisig at 0xff1a8EDA5eAcdB6aAf729905492bdc6376DBe2dd (labeled 'Gnosis: xDai Bridge Management Multisig')
TokenBridge documentation site lists Ethereum Proxy Contract: 0x4aa42145Aa6Ebf72e164C9bBC74fbD3788045016 with xDAI as the bridge asset and Gnosis Chain as destination
No audit links were provided in the protocol definition; unable to extract addresses from audit scope tables
No GitHub repository was provided in the protocol definition; unable to walk deployment artifacts
Single chain (Ethereum mainnet) specified; no cross-chain variants discovered in this run
No factory patterns identified; bridge is a direct ERC20-to-Native token bridge, not a factory-based system
Two role types catalogued: proxy (EternalStorageProxy at 0x4aa42145...) with proxy_admin at management multisig, and multisig (3-of-4 threshold at 0xff1a8EDA...) for governance
All addresses traced to Etherscan block explorer pages and verified TokenBridge documentation

rationale.verdict: Walking Etherscan name-tag search and published bridge documentation yielded 4 contract addresses: 1 proxy, 1 implementation, and 1 multisig governance contract with 4 identified owners, all on Ethereum mainnet.

evidence.shows: xDAI Bridge proxy contract on Ethereum mainnet; labeled 'Gnosis Chain: xDai Bridge'; EternalStorageProxy pattern with implementation at 0x257bDD093cab1bd39ebf837dcb60f33d031d7d49; holds $105M+ in bridge deposits (sUSDS, USDS, cDAI, etc.)

Official TokenBridge documentation specifies xDAI Bridge as ERC20-to-Native bridge; lists Ethereum Proxy Contract: 0x4aa42145Aa6Ebf72e164C9bBC74fbD3788045016; GC Proxy Contract: 0x7301CFA0e1756B71869E93d4e4Dca5c7d0eb0AA6; requires governance for parameter changes

xDAI Bridge Management Multisig on Ethereum mainnet; MultiSigWallet contract (v0.4.15); 4 owners with 3-of-4 required: 0x9ad83402C19Af24F76afa1930a2B2EEC2F47A8C5, 0x4D1c96B9A49C4469A0b720a22b74b034EDdFe051, 0xc073C8E5ED9Aa11CF6776C69b3e13b259Ba9F506, 0x491FC792e78CDadd7D31446Bb7bDDef876a69AD6; 68 transactions on record

Configuration documentation references Bridge Home contract 0x7301cfa0e1756b71869e93d4e4dca5c7d0eb0aa6 (deployed in xDai chain) and multisig wallet 0x0d3726e5a9f37234d6b55216fc971d30f150a60f (xDai chain) for governance transactions

Bridge usage documentation confirms xDAI Bridge address 0x4aa42145Aa6Ebf72e164C9bBC74fbD3788045016 as recipient for DAI deposits on Ethereum mainnet; provides relayTokens and executeSignatures methods interface

unknowns: D2: No deployed-contracts documentation page discovered on gnosis.io main website; TokenBridge docs used as authoritative source instead
D3: No audit links provided in protocol metadata; unable to verify audit-era addresses
D4: No GitHub repository provided; unable to enumerate Foundry/Hardhat deployment artifacts
D5-offchain: Gnosis Chain (xDAI home contract) is on different chain; pinned protocol lists only Ethereum; xDAI home bridge discovered via docs but not fetchable without Gnosis chain added to scope
D6: No factory enumeration performed (no factory pattern identified)

protocol_metadata.about: xDAI Bridge is an ERC20-to-Native token bridge enabling conversion of DAI stablecoins on Ethereum mainnet to xDAI on Gnosis Chain. The bridge locks DAI collateral in a smart contract and mints equivalent xDAI on the destination chain. Bridge governance is administered by a 3-of-4 multisig on Ethereum mainnet with authority to update contract parameters, validator sets, and daily transfer limits.
